moodGreat.desc: Mood option label (mood key 'great'). Shown on the journal mood picker and entry cards.

moodHard.desc: Mood option label (mood key 'hard'). Single word — distinct from the 'Hard day' label used in History.

subMoodAnxious.desc: Journal sub-mood word (lowercase) shown as a selectable chip after a hard/crisis mood. Slug: 'anxious'.

subMoodProud.desc: Journal sub-mood word shown after a 'great' mood. Slug: 'proud'.

journalPromptR1.en: What pulled at me today — and what held me steady?

journalPromptR2.en: If today had a colour, what would it be? Why?

journalPromptR3.en: What did my body tell me today that I almost ignored?

journalPromptR4.en: Where did I show up for myself today, even imperfectly?

journalPromptR5.en: What truth am I avoiding right now?

journalPromptR6.en: What story did I tell myself today — and was it kind, or just familiar?

journalPromptR7.en: What would the wisest version of me say about today?

journalPromptR8.en: What is one thing I am ready to set down?

journalPromptR9.en: What feeling have I been outrunning?

journalPromptR10.en: When did I feel most like myself today?

journalPromptG1.en: Three small things I am grateful for right now.

journalPromptG2.en: Someone who made my life easier this week — and why.

journalPromptG3.en: A body part that did its job today without me noticing.

journalPromptG4.en: A sound, smell, or taste that landed today.

journalPromptG5.en: A thing I have now that past-me would have begged for.

journalPromptG6.en: A small comfort that softened a hard moment.
journalPromptG6.desc: Journal gratitude prompt g6 (also a starter prompt in the empty state).

journalPromptG7.en: A piece of music, a view, a meal — what fed me today?

journalPromptG8.en: Who in my life right now is steady? Name them.

journalPromptG9.en: A skill I have today that I did not have a year ago.

journalPromptG10.en: One ordinary moment today that I want to remember.

journalPromptH1.en: What hurt today? Just name it — no fix, no spin.

journalPromptH2.en: If this feeling could speak, what would it say it needs?

journalPromptH3.en: What part of today felt unfair?

journalPromptH4.en: Is there a feeling I am calling anger that is actually something else underneath?

journalPromptH5.en: What would I say to a friend who was where I am right now?
journalPromptH5.desc: Journal hard-day prompt h5 (also a starter prompt in the empty state).

journalPromptH6.en: What is the smallest next step I can take, even if I do not feel like it?

journalPromptH7.en: Who can I tell about this — even one person, even one sentence?

journalPromptH8.en: What am I making this mean about me — and is that true?

journalPromptH9.en: What did today take from me? What did it leave?

journalPromptH10.en: If I could fast-forward 24 hours, what would I want to be true?

journalPromptW1.en: A moment today I am quietly proud of.

journalPromptW2.en: Something I did today that past-me could not have done.

journalPromptW3.en: A risk I took — however small — and how it landed.

journalPromptW4.en: Where did I choose myself today?

journalPromptW5.en: A boundary I held, even if no one noticed.

journalPromptW7.en: A conversation I am glad I had.

journalPromptW9.en: A way my body felt strong today.

journalPromptW10.en: A way I treated myself the way I would treat someone I love.

journalPromptC1.en: When did the urge start today, and what was happening around me?

journalPromptC2.en: What was my body doing when the craving hit?

journalPromptC3.en: What was the lie the craving was telling me?

journalPromptC4.en: What did I actually need underneath the craving — rest, connection, food, quiet?

journalPromptC5.en: How long did it last before it began to pass?

journalPromptC6.en: What did I do instead — and how do I feel about that choice now?

journalPromptC7.en: Who or what helped me ride this one out?

journalPromptC8.en: If this craving returns tomorrow, what is one thing I can have ready?

journalPromptC9.en: What would the version of me a year sober say to this craving?

journalPromptC10.en: What is the craving costing me, even when I do not use?

journalPromptP1.en: Who do I owe an honest sentence to — even if I never say it?

journalPromptP2.en: A relationship that feels lighter than it did a year ago.

journalPromptP3.en: Someone I keep replaying conversations with — what is unfinished there?

journalPromptP4.en: A person I keep meaning to reach out to — what is one sentence I could send?

journalPromptP5.en: Where do I feel most seen lately? Where do I feel most invisible?

journalPromptP6.en: What is one boundary I am proud of — even a small one?

journalPromptP7.en: Who in my life has earned more of me? Who has earned less?

journalPromptP8.en: A thing someone said to me that I am still carrying.

journalPromptP9.en: What would a healthier version of me say to the people in my life right now?

journalPromptP10.en: Who do I want to be remembered as — by the people closest to me?

visionCategoryUncategorised.desc: Vision-board category label for items with no category (VisionCategory.none).

visionStarterFreedomYearTitle.desc: Vision-board sample/seed dream title (id 'freedom_year'). Pre-fills the title field; the user can edit it.

visionStarterFreedomYearAffirmation.en: I am building a life I love, one sober day at a time.

visionStarterPresentParentTitle.en: Be the parent I want to be

visionStarterPresentParentAffirmation.en: I am present, patient, and proud of how I show up for my family.

visionStarterRun5kAffirmation.en: I am strong, I move with purpose, and my body is reclaiming itself.

visionStarterSaveMeaningfulAffirmation.en: Every day sober is money in my pocket and possibility in my future.

visionStarterLearnSkillAffirmation.en: I am curious, I am capable, and I keep growing.

visionStarterHealRelationshipAffirmation.en: I lead with honesty and humility. The right people are coming closer.

cravingResponseWalked.desc: Craving-response chip label (slug 'walked') — what the user did when a craving hit.

cravingTimeAm.desc: Morning meridiem marker for the craving risk-window time label (e.g. the 'AM' in '8–11 AM').

cravingTimePm.desc: Afternoon/evening meridiem marker for the craving risk-window time label (e.g. the 'PM' in '8–11 PM').

cravingHourMeridiem.desc: A single clock hour with its AM/PM marker, e.g. '8 PM'. Used to build the craving risk-window range.

cravingRiskWindowRange.desc: The user's highest-risk 3-hour craving window as a range, e.g. '8 PM–11 PM'. Separator is an en dash (–). {start} and {end} are each an hour+meridiem from cravingHourMeridiem.

journalReauthUnlockEntry.desc: Default biometric/device-auth prompt reason when re-authenticating to view a locked journal entry.

journalReauthIncorrectPin.desc: Inline error shown under the PIN field in the per-entry unlock dialog when the entered PIN is wrong.
